v1_banner: Closed tournaments are auto-deleted after 14 days. Inactive tournaments are auto-deleted after 31 days.
est_round_duration: Est. round duration: ~{minutes} min per shift
delete_tournament_confirm: Delete this tournament? This cannot be undone.
scoring_override_hint: Override scoring per group type. Changes apply from the next round.
injury_options_label: How would you like to handle the remaining matches?
injury_substitute_desc: Play 2v2 with a temporary substitute. Scores entered normally.
injury_cancel_desc: Cancel remaining matches. Standings use average points.
court_all_done: All groups finished for this round. To retire a player because of injury after scores were entered, wait until the next round opens.
create_names_placeholder_preseed: One name per line, with seed points:
create_wvv_tip: Upload the CSV from "Setzliste" directly, or copy the "spieler 1" and "wvv" columns from a spreadsheet and paste them here. Name and Points separated by comma, semicolon, tab or space are supported.
create_desc_random: First round random placement, then ladder system with flexible rounds
create_desc_preseed: Seeding based on WVV points, recursive splitting rounds
create_desc_single: Single set, first to 21, win by 2
create_desc_bestof3: First to 2 sets (21pt), deciding set to 15, win by 2
create_desc_custom: Set your own point targets and set count
create_virtual_courts_desc: {virtual} groups will be scheduled across {physical} physical courts in batch shifts
create_leftover_5p_format: 2 parallel games per run, 2 runs = 4 games/round
create_leftover_5p_rules: Players rotate every point. One player plays 4 games, others play 3.
create_leftover_6p_format: 2 parallel games per run, 2 runs = 4 games/round
create_leftover_6p_rules: Pairs rotate every point. Some play 3 games, others play 2.
create_leftover_3p_scoring: Same as standard (21 points or per tournament settings)
create_leftover_3p_rules: Each player takes a solo turn against the pair.
court_share_hint: Share this QR code with your group partners
court_closed_message: Scores have been finalized. Stand by for next round assignments.
court_3p_desc: Each player takes a turn solo against the other two in pairs. Everyone plays every possible matchup. First to {points} points, win by 2.
court_substitute_notice: A substitute is playing in place of an injured player. Subs are marked with SUBST.
standings_empty_hint: Complete at least one round to see total standings.
frozen_courts_info: These groups have completed their bracket and their standings are final.
login_prompt: Please log in to manage tournaments.
signup_password_length: Password must be at least 10 characters
err_player_not_in_round: Player is not in the current round
err_injury_scores_entered: Scores have already been entered. Use Report Injury instead.
err_retire_scores_entered: No scores entered yet. Use Retire Player instead.
err_wrong_undo_type_injury: Player was injured, not retired. Use undo injury instead.
err_wrong_undo_type_retire: Player was retired, not injured. Use undo retirement instead.
err_undo_retire_scores_entered: Cannot undo retirement: scores have been entered
err_undo_injury_scores_entered: Cannot undo injury: scores have been entered on this group
err_min_players: At least {count} players required. You entered {entered}.
err_score_invalid: Invalid score: winner must reach {minPoints} with a {winBy}-point lead (or deuce rules apply)
cookie_notice: This site uses essential cookies for authentication and security.
privacy_cookies_desc: This website uses essential cookies that are necessary for authentication and security. These cookies are required for the website to function properly and cannot be disabled.
privacy_cookies_session_desc: Keep you signed in during your visit
privacy_cookies_csrf_desc: Protects against cross-site request forgery attacks
privacy_rights_desc: Under GDPR, you have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data. Contact us if you have any questions about how we handle your data.
privacy_storage_desc: Your account information (email address) is stored securely in our database. We do not share your data with third parties.
privacy_questions_desc: If you have questions about this policy, please contact the tournament organizer.
scheduling_batch_info: Batch shifts · {physical} physical court(s) · {groups} group(s) · {shifts} shift(s)
retire_note: Remove a player before any scores are entered in the current round. Remaining players are redistributed for this round the same way as when advancing between rounds.
injury_note: Handle a player injury mid-round. This only affects the player's current group for this round. Other groups continue normally. At the end of the round, the injured player can either continue (substitute) or be retired.
preseed_retirement_shrink: Shrink — remove player only, court may become 3-player
preseed_retirement_policy_help: Cascade keeps standard 4-player groups at higher levels. Shrink simply removes the player, which may create a 3-player group mid-bracket.
retire_use_replacement: Add a replacement (keep group size, no redistribution)
err_replace_frozen_court: Cannot add a replacement on a frozen group
err_replace_duplicate_name: A player with that name is already on the roster
injury_use_replacement: Add a replacement for next round (keep roster size)
scores_read_only: Scores for this round are read-only.
err_court_read_only: This round is closed — scores cannot be edited.
tie_break_hint: Reorder statistical factors to set priority. Disabled factors are skipped.
tie_break_final_hint: Choose exactly one option to resolve ties that remain after the factors above.
manual_rank_hint: Only players tied on all automatic factors can be reordered here. Use the table to compare their stats.
tie_break_standings_legend: Dashed icons were checked but did not separate players. Colored icons show the deciding factor: gold = won the tie-break, green = middle, blue = lost.
err_manual_rank_invalid: Invalid manual order — only reorder players within the same tie group.
docs_3p_courts_p1: When there are 3 leftover players, they form a 3-player group with a solo rotation format.
docs_3p_courts_p2: Each player takes a turn playing solo against the other two as a pair. This ensures everyone plays every possible matchup.
docs_5p_courts_p1: When there is 1 leftover player (16 mod 4 = 0, but 17 mod 4 = 1), a 5-player group is created.
docs_5p_courts_p2: 5-player groups use a parallel game system with 2 runs of 2 games each. One player plays 4 games while others play 3, with the role randomized each round.
docs_6p_courts_p1: When there are 2 leftover players, a 6-player group is created.
docs_6p_courts_p2: 6-player groups use a parallel game system similar to 5-player groups. Some players play 3 games and others play 2, with roles randomized each round.
docs_basic_concept_p1: King of the Beach (KoB) is a beach volleyball tournament format where players rotate partners each round while competing as individuals. Unlike traditional team-based tournaments, every player is ranked individually based on their performance across multiple rounds.
docs_basic_concept_p2: The system uses a round-robin rotation within each group where every player partners with every other player exactly once (in a 4-player group, this means 3 matches per round: A+B vs C+D, A+C vs B+D, A+D vs B+C). At the end of each round, players are redistributed to new courts based on their finish position, ensuring balanced competition throughout the tournament.
docs_batch_shift_p1: When virtual groups outnumber physical courts, the system schedules them in batch shifts. Each shift plays a subset of virtual groups on the available physical courts.
docs_best_of_3_bullet1: First to 2 sets wins the match
docs_best_of_3_bullet2: Regular sets to 21 points, win by 2
docs_best_of_3_bullet3: Deciding set to 15 points, win by 2
docs_best_of_3_bullet4: No cap on any set — deuce continues indefinitely
docs_best_of_3_p1: First to 2 sets wins. Each regular set is to 21 (win by 2). If a third set is needed, it's played to 15 (win by 2).
docs_configuring_bullet1: Reorder factors by dragging them — higher factors are checked first
docs_configuring_bullet2: Disable factors you don't want to use
docs_configuring_bullet3: Choose the final tie-breaker (dice or manual) for ties that all enabled factors couldn't resolve
docs_configuring_tie_breaks_p1: Tournament organizers can configure the tie-break rules from the tournament dashboard:
docs_custom_bullet1: Set any number of sets to win
docs_custom_bullet2: Set any regular set point target
docs_custom_bullet3: Set any deciding set point target
docs_custom_p1: Set your own point targets, set count, and deciding set points. Full flexibility for experimental or house-rule formats.
docs_deuce_aware_validation_p1: When entering scores, the system validates that the winner reached the minimum points with the required win-by margin. Deuce rules are handled automatically.
docs_deuce_aware_validation_p2: If scores are tied at or above the target, play continues until one team leads by 2. The system accepts any valid deuce score.
docs_example_tournament_p1: Here's how a typical 16-player tournament (4 groups, 4 rounds) works:
docs_factor_dice_desc: Random dice roll — fair but unpredictable
docs_factor_manual_desc: Organizer can manually reorder tied players
docs_factor_round_diff_desc: Point differential in the current round only
docs_factor_round_points_desc: Points scored in the current round only
docs_factor_total_diff_desc: Total point differential across all rounds
docs_factor_total_points_desc: Total points scored across all rounds
docs_footer: King of the Beach — the fair way to run beach volleyball tournaments.
docs_format_preseed_p1: Players are seeded by their WVV points (or any numeric seed) using a snake draft. The tournament then follows a fixed bracket with recursive binary splits — similar to a tennis tournament bracket but with groups instead of eliminations.
docs_format_preseed_p2: Each round splits every bracket into a winner half (1st+2nd placements) and a loser half (3rd+4th placements). This continues until each group reaches its final position.
docs_format_random_seed_p1: The first round is shuffled randomly. After that, players are sorted by finish position within their group and redistributed using vertical seeding (Round 1→2) followed by a ladder system (Round 2+). This format is flexible about round count and works well for casual tournaments.
docs_icon_deciding: Deciding — this factor is currently the one separating players
docs_icon_lost: Lost — player lost on this factor
docs_icon_middle: Middle — player placed mid on this factor
docs_icon_tied: Tied — factor checked but did not separate players
docs_icon_won: Won — player won on this factor
docs_lead: Everything you need to know about running a King of the Beach beach volleyball tournament.
docs_per_court_overrides_p1: Organizers can override the scoring mode for specific group types (e.g., use Best of 3 on standard 4-player groups but Single Set on 5-player or 6-player groups).
docs_per_court_overrides_p2: Overrides take effect from the next round. They do not retroactively change already-played matches.
docs_preseed_bracket_p1: In Preseed format, redistribution follows the bracket tree. Each bracket splits recursively based on finish positions within the bracket.
docs_preseed_bracket_tree_p1: The bracket tree is determined by the number of courts. Each round, every bracket of size N splits into two equal halves (Winners and Losers). Courts that complete their bracket path early become frozen.
docs_preseed_frozen_courts_p1: Frozen groups have completed their bracket path and will not be redistributed further. Their standings are final.
docs_preseed_frozen_courts_p2: The freeze cascade depends on the group count. Lower groups tend to freeze earlier as they complete their bracket leaves.
docs_principle_court_position: Group position determines redistribution — finish higher, move up
docs_principle_individual_ranking: Individual ranking — your score follows you, not your partner
docs_principle_partner_rotation: Partner rotation — you play with and against everyone
docs_principle_stable_tokens: Stable court tokens — share a QR code so groups can self-report scores
docs_random_seed_ladder_p1: After Round 2, the ladder system moves 2 players up and 2 players down between adjacent groups.
docs_round_1_2_vertical_seeding_p1: After Round 1, all players are grouped by their finish position (all 1st places together, all 2nd places together, etc.). Within each tier, players are sorted by points descending, then by point differential descending. The flattened list is then distributed across groups in order.
docs_round_2plus_ladder_p1: After Round 2, a ladder system takes effect. The top 2 players from each group move up (or stay), while the bottom 2 move down (or stay). This creates a continuous promotion/relegation dynamic.
docs_single_set_bullet3: No cap — deuce continues indefinitely until a 2-point lead is achieved
docs_single_set_p1: One set played to a target score (default 21, win by 2). Simple and fast — the most common choice for beach volleyball tournaments.
docs_standard_4p_p1: The standard configuration. 4 players play 3 matches per round, with each player partnering every other player exactly once.
docs_tie_break_5p6p_p1: Non-standard group sizes (5-player and 6-player) use normalized scoring to ensure fairness compared to standard 4-player groups.
docs_tie_break_5p6p_p2: Round points are averaged per game played, and total points are normalized by dividing by 3 (the standard number of games per round).
docs_tie_break_icons_p1: In the standings, each factor shows an icon indicating its role in resolving the tie:
docs_tie_break_overview_p1: Tie-breaking determines the final ordering of players within a group when they have the same total points. The system uses a configurable chain of factors to resolve ties.
docs_timing_note: Actual duration varies based on skill level and how quickly games are played. Times assume continuous play with minimal delays.
docs_tip1: Print the QR codes for each group and tape them to the net post — players scan to enter their own scores.
docs_tip2: Use a tournament-wide WhatsApp group or Telegram channel for announcements.
docs_tip3: Keep rounds tight with a visible timer. Announce 5-minute and 1-minute warnings.
docs_tip4: Have a substitution list ready for injured players.
docs_tip5: For large tournaments (20+ players), assign a dedicated scorekeeper.
docs_tip6: Use the wait time forecast to manage player expectations across shifts.
docs_tip7: Prepare the final group with a podium area for the award ceremony.
docs_tournament_day_p1: A typical 16-player tournament with 4 groups on 4 physical courts runs in about 4 hours 30 minutes.
docs_two_groups_one_court_p1: When you have more groups than physical courts, groups can alternate on the same court.
docs_two_groups_one_court_p2: Two approaches: traditional sequential (one group fully finishes, then the next) or alternating (matches interleaved for better flow).
docs_virtual_courts_p1: Overview of how virtual groups are scheduled across physical courts using batch shifts.
docs_wait_time_p1: The system estimates wait times for players based on their shift position. Players in later shifts can see how long until their group plays.
faq_a_3p_5p_6p_courts: When player counts don't divide evenly by 4, the system creates non-standard groups. 3p groups use a solo rotation format. 5p and 6p groups use parallel game systems with runs and alternating partners.
faq_a_best_of_3_deciding: The deciding set (if needed) is played to 15 points instead of 21, still win by 2. This keeps the match length reasonable while maintaining fairness.
faq_a_can_i_export: There is no built-in export feature yet. You can view standings on screen and take a screenshot. Export to CSV or PDF is planned for a future update.
faq_a_change_format: No, the format is locked once the tournament starts. Choose carefully before the first round. You can, however, adjust round count and scoring settings between rounds.
faq_a_court_labels: Yes! During tournament creation, you can assign custom labels to your physical courts (e.g., 'Court A', 'Main Court', 'Beach 1'). These labels appear in the scheduling view.
faq_a_customize_tie_breaks: Yes. From the tournament dashboard, open Tie-Break Rules. You can reorder factors by priority, disable factors, and choose the final tie-breaker (dice or manual).
faq_a_data_stored: The organizer's email address and player names are stored in the database. Player names are only visible within their tournament. We do not store cookies for tracking or advertising.
faq_a_deuce_rules: There is no cap. If the score reaches the target (e.g., 20-20), play continues until one team leads by 2 points. Scores like 22-20, 25-23, or 30-28 are all valid.
faq_a_do_players_need_accounts: No! Only the tournament organizer needs an account. Players access their group page through a QR code — no login required. They just scan, enter scores, and go.
faq_a_frozen_court: A frozen group is a group that has completed its bracket path in Preseed format. Its standings are final and it won't be redistributed further. Lower groups tend to freeze earlier in the tournament.
faq_a_how_5p_parallel: 5 players play 4 games across 2 runs. In each run, there are 2 parallel games. One player ends up playing 4 games while others play 3. The role of the 4-game player is randomized each round.
faq_a_how_6p_parallel: 6 players play 4 games across 2 runs. Some players play 3 games and others play 2. Roles are randomized each round. The partnership rule ensures no pair plays together in both runs.
faq_a_how_many_players: You need at least 8 players (2 groups) and at most 64 players (16 groups). The format works best with multiples of 4, but the system also handles 3, 5, and 6-player groups when player counts don't divide evenly.
faq_a_how_many_rounds: For Random Seed, you can choose any number of rounds (minimum 2). The system recommends a default based on your player count. For Preseed, the number of rounds is fixed by the bracket tree — typically 3 rounds for 16 players (4 groups) or 4 rounds for 32 players (8 groups).
faq_a_how_standings: Players are ranked by: Total Points → Point Differential → Player ID (deterministic). For non-standard groups (5p, 6p), scores are normalized. Tie-break factors are configurable and can include round points, round diff, total points, total diff, seeding, dice, or manual ordering.
faq_a_how_to_create_tournament: Sign up for an account, click '+ New Tournament', enter player names, choose your format (Random Seed or Preseed), configure scoring, and submit. The system generates QR codes for each group that you can print and share.
faq_a_how_winner: The winner is the 1st place player in Group 1 (the top group) after the final round. In Preseed format, the winner is the player who finishes 1st in the Gold bracket. Total points are used for redistribution but the final group position determines the champion.
faq_a_override_scoring: Yes. From the tournament dashboard, you can override the scoring mode for specific group types. For example, use Best of 3 on standard groups and Single Set on 5-player groups. Overrides apply from the next round.
faq_a_privacy: Player names are stored temporarily for the duration of the tournament. See the Privacy page for full details on data handling and your GDPR rights.
faq_a_random_vs_preseed: Random Seed shuffles players randomly for the first round, then uses a ladder system (2 up, 2 down) for subsequent rounds. It's flexible about round count. Preseed uses player ratings for a snake draft, then follows a fixed bracket tree with recursive splits. Preseed is more structured while Random Seed is more casual and flexible.
faq_a_scoring_modes: Three modes: Single Set (first to 21, win by 2), Best of 3 (first to 2 sets of 21, deciding set to 15), and Custom (set your own targets). You can also override scoring per group type.
faq_a_tie_break_factors: The system supports 7 factors: Round Points, Round Diff, Total Points, Total Diff, Initial Order (seeding), Dice (random), and Manual (organizer decides). You can reorder and enable/disable them.
faq_a_tie_break_icons: Dashed icons mean the factor was checked but didn't separate players. Colored icons show the deciding factor: gold = player won, green = middle, blue = lost on that factor.
faq_a_total_standings: The Total Standings page shows the complete ranking of all players, winners, tournament achievements (Most Improved, Consistent Performer, Group Champion), and retired players with their reasons.
faq_a_tournament_deleted: Closed tournaments are auto-deleted after 14 days. Inactive tournaments are auto-deleted after 31 days. You can also delete a tournament manually at any time.
faq_a_two_groups_one_court: Yes. When groups outnumber physical courts, they alternate. You can choose between sequential (one group finishes, then the next) or alternating (matches interleaved for better flow and shorter total time).
faq_a_virtual_vs_physical: Virtual groups are groups in the system. Physical courts are actual beach volleyball courts. When virtual groups outnumber physical ones, groups are scheduled in batch shifts — some groups play while others wait.
faq_a_what_browser: The app works on all modern browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Safari, and Edge. Mobile browsers are fully supported since players often access group pages via QR codes on their phones.
faq_a_what_is_kob: King of the Beach is a beach volleyball tournament format where players rotate partners each round. Everyone plays with and against everyone else. Rankings are individual — your performance follows you, not your partner. The system automatically handles group assignments, scoring, and standings.
faq_a_which_format: Choose Random Seed for casual tournaments, flexible schedules, or when players don't have seed points. Choose Preseed for competitive tournaments with known player ratings (e.g., WVV points), or when you want a more structured bracket system with frozen groups.
faq_a_why_5p_6p_15: Non-standard groups play more games per round (4 games instead of 3). To keep round duration consistent, the default point target is lowered to 15. You can change this in Custom mode.
faq_a_works_offline: No, the app requires an internet connection. Scores are submitted to the server in real time. However, the mobile interface is optimized for beach-side use with large buttons and high contrast.
faq_footer: More questions? Contact your tournament organizer.
faq_lead: Common questions about running KoB tournaments.
faq_q_best_of_3_deciding_set: How does the deciding set work in Best of 3?
faq_q_can_i_change_format_mid_tournament: Can I change the format mid-tournament?
faq_q_can_i_override_scoring_per_court: Can I set different scoring for different groups?
faq_q_can_i_set_court_labels: Can I label my physical courts?
faq_q_how_many_players: How many players do I need?
faq_q_how_many_rounds: How many rounds will we play?
faq_q_how_to_create_tournament: How do I create a tournament?
faq_q_how_winner_determined: How is the tournament winner determined?
faq_q_random_vs_preseed: What's the difference between Random Seed and Preseed?
faq_q_tie_break_icons: What do the tie-break icons mean?
faq_q_total_standings_page: What information is on the total standings page?
faq_q_two_groups_one_court: Can two groups share one physical court?
faq_q_virtual_vs_physical: What's the difference between virtual and physical courts?
faq_q_what_is_kob: What is King of the Beach (KoB)?
faq_q_why_5p_6p_default_15: Why do 5-player and 6-player groups default to 15 points?
docs_diagram_round_progression:
Round 1:    [C1] [C2] [C3] [C4]  (random shuffle)
Round 2:    [C1] [C2] [C3] [C4]  (vertical seeding → ladder)
Round 3:    [C1] [C2] [C3] [C4]  (ladder: 2 up, 2 down)
Round 4:    [C1] [C2] [C3] [C4]  (ladder → Final)

16p is the special case where each tier (4 players) fills exactly one court.
		Winner = 1st place on Court 1 after final round.
docs_diagram_vertical_seeding:
Algorithm:
1. Group all players by finish position (1sts, 2nds, 3rds, 4ths)
2. Sort each tier by configured tie-breaking rules
3. Flatten: [1sts..., 2nds..., 3rds..., 4ths...]
4. Fill courts sequentially: top players → Court 1, next → Court 2, etc.

16 players (4 courts): Each tier has exactly 4 players
→ Court 1 = All 1st place (sorted)
→ Court 2 = All 2nd place (sorted)
→ Court 3 = All 3rd place (sorted)
→ Court 4 = All 4th place (sorted)

20 players (5 courts): Tiers cross boundaries
→ 1sts: top 4 → C1, 5th → C2
→ 2nds: top 3 → C2, next 2 → C3
→ 3rds: top 2 → C3, next 2 → C4, last → C5
→ 4ths: top 1 → C4, next 3 → C5
docs_diagram_ladder:
Court 1: Keep 1st & 2nd, add 1st & 2nd from Court 2
Court 2: Add 3rd & 4th from Court 1, add 1st & 2nd from Court 3
Court 3: Add 3rd & 4th from Court 2, add 1st & 2nd from Court 4
Court 4: Keep 3rd & 4th, add 3rd & 4th from Court 3

Same logic extends for any court count ≥ 2.
docs_diagram_preseed_bracket:
16 Players (4 Courts, 3 Rounds):
Round 1:    [C1] [C2] [C3] [C4]     (snake seeding)
              ↓
Round 2:    [W1] [W2] | [L1] [L2]  (splitSize(4)=2 → 2W+2L, origin mixing)
              ↓          ↓
Round 3:  [F] [L(W)] | [TL] [BL]   (each bracket halves: 2→1F+1L(W), 2→1TL+1BL)

Winner bracket (C1,C2): 1sts+2nds → Court 1 (Gold), 3rds+4ths → Court 2 (Silver)
Loser bracket (C3,C4):  1sts+2nds → Court 3 (Bronze), 3rds+4ths → Court 4 (Iron)

32 Players (8 Courts, 4 Rounds):
R1→R2: splitSize(8)=4 → 4W + 4L
R2→R3: Each half of 8 splits independently (splitSize(8)=4 → 4W+4L in winners, same in losers)
R3→R4: Each quarter of 4 splits (splitSize(4)=2 → 2W+2L)
docs_diagram_frozen_courts:
Cascade freeze points (examples):
3 courts:  C3 after R2 (3→2W+1L, winners halve at R3)
4 courts:  C4 after R3 (4→2+2, then 2→1+1)
5 courts:  C5 after R2 (5→4+1, overflow freezes), C1-C4 after R4
7 courts:  C7 after R3 (7→4+3, losers 3→2+1), C1-C6 after R4
8 courts:  C8 after R4 (8→4+4, then 4→2+2, then 2→1+1)
16 courts: C16 after R5 (16→8+8, 8→4+4, 4→2+2, 2→1+1)

Frozen courts complete their bracket leaves early and don't participate in further splits.
docs_diagram_4p:
3 matches per round:
Match 1: P1 + P2 vs P3 + P4
Match 2: P1 + P3 vs P2 + P4
Match 3: P1 + P4 vs P2 + P3

Ranking: Total points → Diff → playerId
docs_diagram_3p:
3 players: A, B, C
3 matches per round:
Match 1: A + B vs C
Match 2: A + C vs B
Match 3: B + C vs A

Solo player uses all 3 touches
Scoring: Same as 4p (default 21, win by 2)
Ranking: Total points → Diff → playerId (same games per player)

Timing: ~48 min (3 × 14 + 2 × 3 min)
docs_diagram_5p:
5 Players (A, B, C, D, E):
2 runs × 2 parallel games = 4 games total

Games 1 & 2 (Run 1) play interleaved: D and E swap every point.
Each point belongs to either Game 1 or Game 2.
The waiting player tracks their game's score AND service rotation.

RUN 1:
Side X: A+B stay on court (fixed team)
Side Y: C stays on court (fixed), D and E alternate every point
Game 1: A+B vs C+D (D on court, E tracks Game 2 off court)
Game 2: A+B vs C+E (E on court, D tracks Game 1 off court)

RUN 2:
Side X: D+E stay on court (fixed team)
Side Y: B stays on court (fixed), A and C alternate every point
Game 3: D+E vs B+A (A on court, C tracks Game 4 off court)
Game 4: D+E vs B+C (C on court, A tracks Game 3 off court)

Game count per player:
A: 3 games  |  B: 4 games  |  C: 3 games  |  D: 3 games  |  E: 3 games
(One player plays 4 games, role randomized each round)
docs_diagram_6p:
6 Players (A, B, C, D, E, F):
2 runs × 2 parallel games = 4 games total

Games 1 & 2 (Run 1) play interleaved: pairs swap every point.
Each point belongs to either Game 1 or Game 2.
The waiting pair tracks their game's score AND service rotation.

RUN 1:
Side X: A+B stay on court (fixed team)
Side Y: C+D and E+F rotate every point (pairs alternate)
Game 1: A+B vs C+D (C+D on court, E+F tracks Game 2 off court)
Game 2: A+B vs E+F (E+F on court, C+D tracks Game 1 off court)

RUN 2:
Side X: C+D stay on court (fixed team)
Side Y: A+B and E+F rotate every point (pairs alternate)
Game 3: C+D vs A+B (A+B on court, E+F tracks Game 4 off court)
Game 4: C+D vs E+F (E+F on court, A+B tracks Game 3 off court)

Game count per player:
A: 3  |  B: 3  |  C: 3  |  D: 3  |  E: 2  |  F: 2
(Role randomized each round. Partnership rule: no pair partners in both runs)

Ranking: Average points per round → Total points → Diff → playerId
docs_diagram_16p_ladder:
ROUND 2 (Vertical Seeding) — 16p special case:
1sts (4) → Court 1
2nds (4) → Court 2
3rds (4) → Court 3
4ths (4) → Court 4

ROUND 3+ (Ladder):
C1 ← C1[1+2] + C2[1+2]   (top 2 stay, pull top 2 from below)
C2 ← C1[3+4] + C3[1+2]   (bottom 2 drop, pull top 2 from below)
C3 ← C2[3+4] + C4[1+2]   (bottom 2 drop, pull top 2 from below)
C4 ← C3[3+4] + C4[3+4]   (bottom 2 stay, pull bottom 2 from above)

Visual flow:
Round 2:  C1(1sts)  C2(2nds)  C3(3rds)  C4(4ths)
Round 3:  C1[C1↑+C2↑]  C2[C1↓+C3↑]  C3[C2↓+C4↑]  C4[C3↓+C4↓]
docs_diagram_20p_ladder:
ROUND 2 (Vertical Seeding):
1sts (5):  C1×4, C2×1
2nds (5):  C2×3, C3×2
3rds (5):  C3×2, C4×2, C5×1
4ths (5):  C4×1, C5×4

ROUND 3 (Ladder):
C1 ← C1[1+2] + C2[1+2]
C2 ← C1[3+4] + C2[3+4] + C3[1+2]  (3 courts → 4 players from 3 sources)
C3 ← C3[3+4] + C4[1+2]
C4 ← C4[3+4] + C5[1+2]
C5 ← C5[3+4] + (empty)  (bottom court keeps bottom 2, no court below)

Non-standard bottom court (C5) always gets remaining players.
docs_diagram_16p_preseed:
ROUND 1 (Snake Seeding):
C1: A(1600) H(900)  I(800)  P(100)
C2: B(1500) G(1000) J(700)  O(200)
C3: C(1400) F(1100) K(600)  N(300)
C4: D(1300) E(1200) L(500)  M(400)

R1 Results → R2 (First Split, splitSize(4)=2):
Tiers:  1sts: A,B,C,E  2nds: D,F,J,H  3rds: G,L,N,I  4ths: K,P,M,O
Winner bracket (top 8):  A(C1), B(C2), C(C3), E(C4), D(C4), F(C3), J(C2), H(C1)
Loser bracket (bottom 8): G(C2), L(C4), N(C3), I(C1), K(C3), P(C1), M(C4), O(C2)

Origin mixing in Winners:
C1: A(C1), C(C3), D(C4), J(C2)   ← no 1st+2nd from same origin
C2: B(C2), E(C4), F(C3), H(C1)

R2 Results → R3 (Subsequent Split):
Winner bracket (C1,C2): 1sts+2nds → C1(Gold), 3rds+4ths → C2(Silver)
Loser bracket (C3,C4):  1sts+2nds → C3(Bronze), 3rds+4ths → C4(Iron)

Final Courts:
C1(F): C, B, A, H
C2(LW): D, E, F, J
C3(TL): G, L, N, O
C4(BL): I, M, K, P

Final Standings:
1st B, 2nd A, 3rd C, 4th H (Court 1)
5th D, 6th E, 7th J, 8th F (Court 2)
9th G, 10th N, 11th L, 12th O (Court 3)
13th I, 14th K, 15th M, 16th P (Court 4)
docs_diagram_8v4:
8 virtual courts, 4 physical courts:

Shift 1: Virtual courts 5-8 → Physical courts 1-4 (simultaneous)
[WAIT]   Score entry, redistribution, rest (~10 min transition)
Shift 2: Virtual courts 1-4 → Physical courts 1-4 (simultaneous)

Order: Bottom courts first (highest numbers), top court last.
This ensures championship match is the last match of the tournament.
docs_diagram_12v4:
12 virtual courts, 4 physical courts:

Shift 1: Virtual courts  9-12 → Physical courts 1-4
[WAIT]   ~10 min transition
Shift 2: Virtual courts  5-8  → Physical courts 1-4
[WAIT]   ~10 min transition
Shift 3: Virtual courts  1-4  → Physical courts 1-4

Total shifts = ceil(12/4) = 3
Round duration ≈ 3 × 45 min + 2 × 10 min = 155 min
docs_diagram_wait_time:
Player on Virtual Court 5 (Shift 2 of 3):
- Remaining shifts after theirs: 1 (Shift 3)
- Avg court duration: 45 min
- Transition time: 10 min
- Their shift duration: 45 min
- Est. total from now: ~100 min
docs_diagram_traditional_alternating:
TRADITIONAL (sequential groups):
Group A plays all matches → break → Group B plays all matches
Group A: Match 1, Match 2, Match 3 (≈45 min)
Break: 10 min
Group B: Match 1, Match 2, Match 3 (≈45 min)
Total: ~100 min for both groups on one physical court

ALTERNATING (interleaved groups):
Match 1: Group A
Match 2: Group B
Match 3: Group A
Match 4: Group B
Match 5: Group A
Match 6: Group B
Total: ~90 min (no dedicated break between groups, natural rotation)

Benefits:
- Shorter total duration (no 10-min inter-group break)
- Players get natural rest while other group plays
- No "cold" start for Group B
- Fairer — both groups progress simultaneously
- Keeps all players engaged
docs_diagram_timeline:
TYPICAL 16-PLAYER TOURNAMENT (4 courts, 4 physical):
08:30  Setup, check-in, briefing
09:00  Round 1 starts (all 4 courts)
09:45  Round 1 ends, scores finalized
09:55  Redistribution, player break
10:00  Round 2 starts
10:45  Round 2 ends
10:55  Break
11:00  Round 3 starts
11:45  Round 3 ends
11:55  Break
12:00  Round 4 (Final) starts
12:45  Tournament ends, podium
13:00  Cleanup

Total: ~4h 30min

WITH VIRTUAL COURTS (8 virtual, 4 physical):
Round 1: Shift 1 (VC5-8) 09:00-09:45 → Shift 2 (VC1-4) 09:55-10:40
Round 2: Shift 1 10:50-11:35 → Shift 2 11:45-12:30
Round 3: Shift 1 12:40-13:25 → Shift 2 13:35-14:20
Round 4: Shift 1 14:30-15:15 → Shift 2 15:25-16:10

Total: ~7h 10min
docs_diagram_5p6p_normalization:
Round points (5p/6p):
round_points = sum(matchPoints) / gamesPlayed

Total points (5p/6p):
Each round contributes roundRawPoints / 3
(3 = standard games per round, regardless of games actually played)

Example 5-player court:
Player A: 3 games, 39 pts → avg 13.0
Player B: 4 games, 48 pts → avg 12.0
Rank: A > B (higher average) even though B has more total points
